heuristics.scenarioLabel: Early position wants a pat seven-or-eight or a hand that is one card away from strong
heuristics.appliesWhen: Opening round in early position before the first draw in 2-7 Triple Draw
heuristics.adjustWhen: Treat discard-two opens as rare exceptions rather than default continues
heuristics.rationale: The source explicitly says early position should start from a pat seven/eight or a hand that is one card away from strong.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Early-position discard-two raises are limited to wheel-heavy starts such as 234 through 238
heuristics.appliesWhen: You would need to discard two cards from early position on the opening round
heuristics.adjustWhen: Hands outside 234, 235, 236, 237, or 238 should generally not be raised from early position
heuristics.rationale: The source gives an explicit early-position whitelist instead of a vague preference for smooth discard-two hands.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: When folded to the button, late-position pressure can override raw card quality
heuristics.appliesWhen: Action folds to you on the button in a late-position opening spot
heuristics.adjustWhen: Expect blinds to fold often enough that immediate fold equity is part of the line
heuristics.rationale: The source states that button opens can be raised regardless of cards because the blinds fold often enough.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: With position, decide whether a nine low stands pat by watching how many cards villain draws
heuristics.appliesWhen: You hold a rough pat nine such as 9-7-5-3-2 and act after seeing the opponent draw
heuristics.adjustWhen: Consider standing pat when the opponent draws two or more cards
heuristics.rationale: The source uses a nine-low example to show that draw-count visibility should directly change the keep-or-break decision.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Out of position in one-card versus one-card final-round spots, polarize between strong hands and total misses
heuristics.appliesWhen: Heads-up or near-heads-up third-draw spot out of position
heuristics.adjustWhen: Bet total garbage that cannot win at showdown
heuristics.rationale: The source gives an explicit final-round polar strategy instead of a generic reminder to bluff sometimes.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Against super-passive opponents, widen the bluff bucket and shrink the check-call bucket
heuristics.appliesWhen: Final betting round in one-card versus one-card spots against a very passive opponent
heuristics.adjustWhen: If villain rarely bets without a monster, convert more weak showdown hands into bluffs
heuristics.rationale: The source explicitly carves out a passive-opponent adjustment rather than leaving the final-round plan static.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: When both players stand pat on the second draw and villain checks, bet almost always
heuristics.appliesWhen: A player stood pat on the second draw
heuristics.adjustWhen: Bet your very strong made lows for value
heuristics.rationale: The source recommends near-auto-betting in this exact pat-versus-pat check-to-you configuration.

heuristics.scenarioLabel: Probe-betting rough made hands can push worse pats into folding or breaking and simplify the river
heuristics.appliesWhen: You hold a rough made hand such as a bad eight, nine, or ten after both players stand pat
heuristics.adjustWhen: Expect some worse pat tens to fold or call and break
heuristics.rationale: The source highlights both immediate fold equity and the downstream river simplification gained by betting rough made hands here.
